text: parents to buy textbooks from school i am a parent in childs private school requires that all parents purchase everything including textbooks directly from the school say it ensures uniformity but the prices are much higher than in local bookstores i asked if i could buy the same books elsewhere they refused saying my child would not be allowed to attend classes without the schoolissued books feels unfair and exploitative ive heard about competition law in when i watched a news program on something similar this practice be considered a breach of that law rights do parents have in such situations em concern is valid you described is a practice that restricts consumer choice or forces customers to buy products from a specific supplier known as a tying practice occurs when the purchase of one product school services is conditional on the purchase of another product textbooks from the same supplier practices can amount to abuse of market power especially if the school is the only provider of education in your area r e governs all matters concerning trade competition in by restricting anticompetitive practices also establishes the fcc which has the authority to investigate and penalise institutions engaging in restrictive trade practices have the right to choose where to buy textbooks the school insists otherwise you can lodge a complaint with the fcc must not exploit their position to force purchases at inflated prices practice undermines competition and consumer welfare your lawyer for further guidance for return of stolen property i am a modern beauty salon owner based in es salon was famous for its elegant interior design and bespoke services monthwe had an unfortunate incident and my beauty salon was mistakenly targeted surveillance camera did not capture anything as the people who took items from the salon were wearing masks i have reported the incident to the police but so far we have not found anything the hope of finding my lost property i have posted posters at the front of the salon offering a monetary reward for the return of the lost items a local government official removed the posters because the law does not allow such a practice i think this is unfair i have lost hope please guide me hs es it is true the law does not permit such kind of offers because they may increase incidences of theft of cap re states that any person who a publicly offers a reward for the return of any property which has been stolen or lost and in the offer makes use of any words purporting that no questions will be asked or that the person producing such property will not be seized or molested b publicly offers to return to any person who may have bought or advanced money by way of loan upon any stolen or lost property the money so paid or advanced or any other sum of money or reward for the return of the property or c prints or publishes any offer referred to in paragraph a or b is guilty of an offence aim of this provision is to prevent a moral hazard the thief or a person who has bought stolen property sends the wrong message to the public are sorry about the loss you have suffered and we hope you recover soon further know is this legal under law a landlord force tenants to pay extra money outside the agreed rent protection do tenants have guide me kl have not reviewed your lease agreement under law particularly the re and the of re a landlord cannot impose charges outside the agreed tenancy contract amount of rent defined in the lease agreement is the only one recognised by law and any additional demands
